prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a black screen.

After the opening black frame, the scene cuts to a black-and-white medium shot of a woman indoors. She faces the camera directly and appears to be speaking or presenting, though no audio content can be inferred from the frames. She has styled dark hair, wears a light-colored blouse or dress with buttons and a necklace, and holds one hand raised near her shoulder. The background suggests a domestic interior, possibly a kitchen or living area, with indistinct furnishings or wall features behind her.

The commercial then cuts to a product display in a living-room setting. A large can of “klear. FLOOR WAX” appears prominently on the left side of the frame. Text on the can includes “New Formula,” “klear.,” “FLOOR WAX,” and “WON’T YELLOW ANY FLOOR.” The background shows a polished floor, seating, lamps, curtains, shelving, and decorative objects, creating an upscale domestic living-room environment.

Large superimposed text appears across the center-right of the frame reading “NEW FORMULA KLEAR” in bold capital letters. The final frame maintains the same product-and-text composition: the floor wax can remains on the left, the large “NEW FORMULA KLEAR” text remains centered to the right, and the living-room interior continues in the background.
